summary: Replayed ADR 0288 from exact latest-main source `2b04836bc154ee415b0e84427973a14ebe15da59`, converged the private Crawl4AI runtime on `docker-runtime-lv3`, restored it through a second governed converge after a concurrent clean stop left the live container exited during receipt capture, verified local health, monitor, markdown extraction, guest-network reachability from `coolify-lv3`, and the dedicated compose-managed `crawl4ai_runtime` bridge, then re-validated the final repository version `0.177.115` against platform version `0.130.75` on top of the latest realistic `origin/main` baseline `0.177.114 / 0.130.74`.

verification.observed: `uv run --with pytest --with pyyaml pytest -q tests/test_crawl4ai_runtime_role.py tests/test_crawl4ai_metadata.py tests/test_docker_runtime_role.py tests/test_common_docker_bridge_chains_helper.py tests/test_generate_platform_vars.py` returned `60 passed in 3.71s` on the exact `0.177.115` tree from source commit `2b04836bc154ee415b0e84427973a14ebe15da59`.
`make syntax-check-crawl4ai` passed on the exact `0.177.115` tree.

verification.check: Authoritative live replay and rebased recovery replay
verification.observed: The exact-main replay succeeded from committed source `2b04836bc154ee415b0e84427973a14ebe15da59`; later, while gathering independent proofs, the live `crawl4ai` container was left cleanly stopped by an external SIGTERM, and a second governed `make converge-crawl4ai` restored service and reran the role-local verification tasks with recap `docker-runtime-lv3 : ok=113 changed=6 unreachable=0 failed=0 skipped=27 rescued=1 ignored=0`.

verification.check: Independent local and guest-network runtime proofs
verification.observed: Independent runbook-style probes returned healthy local `/health` and `/monitor/health` payloads on `docker-runtime-lv3`, a successful markdown extraction for `https://example.com/`, a healthy guest-to-guest `/health` response from `coolify-lv3`, and Docker inspection showed the `crawl4ai` container attached only to the dedicated `crawl4ai_runtime` bridge with host port `11235/tcp` published on both IPv4 and IPv6.

verification.check: Final repository automation and validation bundle
verification.observed: The final `0.177.115 / 0.130.75` tree passed `git diff --check`, `scripts/live_apply_receipts.py --validate`, `./scripts/validate_repo.sh agent-standards workstream-surfaces generated-vars generated-docs health-probes`, full `make validate`, hosted `make remote-validate`, and hosted `make pre-push-gate`. The integrated change surface keeps the Bash 3.2 empty-array expansion repair in `scripts/validate_repo.sh`, Python 3.9 callback compatibility in `scripts/mutation_audit.py` and the structured-log callback, the mirrored `release_tracks.platform_versioning.current` stack field, and the Crawl4AI startup failure classifier for Docker `DOCKER` iptables-chain drift.

notes: ADR 0288 is recorded as implemented in repository version `0.177.115` and first verified live in platform version `0.130.75`.
The compose-managed `crawl4ai_runtime` bridge is part of the success boundary because it removes reliance on Docker's legacy default `bridge` after the guest firewall refresh.
The latest realistic baseline before this replay was `origin/main` repository version `0.177.114` with platform version `0.130.74`.

The validation-path repair is part of the committed surface for this workstream: `scripts/validate_repo.sh`, `scripts/mutation_audit.py`, the structured-log callback plugin, the mirrored `release_tracks.platform_versioning.current` field, and the Crawl4AI runtime startup classifier were updated so the repository's own local and hosted automation gates can execute end to end on the current macOS Bash and Python 3.9/3.12 toolchains.

During independent proof capture, the live `crawl4ai` container received a clean external SIGTERM and remained stopped because Docker treats that path as operator intent even under `restart: unless-stopped`; rerunning the governed playbook from the same exact tree restored service and re-verified the runtime.
The workstream registry remains `ready_for_merge` on the workstream branch so branch-local ownership validation passes; the final fast-forward commit on `main` flips it to `merged`.
